text.title: QA Release Candidate Smoke Pack Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the qa-rc-smoke-pack-template methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale; the final rule is the skip-this-methodology gate referenced by the decision tree.

rule.statement: Smoke pack has ≤10 steps; longer packs delay promotion and are partially executed.
rule.rationale: Beyond ~10 steps the pack stops being a smoke and becomes a slow regression run.

rule.statement: End-to-end pack runs in ≤5 minutes wall-clock on the RC environment.
rule.rationale: If it takes longer, humans cut steps; bounded runtime ensures full execution.

rule.statement: Each step is one runnable command (curl, pytest, k6 single shot) producing a binary pass/fail.
rule.rationale: Manual click-through varies between runs; scripted output is auditable.

rule.statement: Steps cover auth, payment, primary write, primary read, and primary export at minimum. Missing any blocks promotion.
rule.rationale: Smoke without the top 5 paths gives false confidence.

rule.statement: Pack file carries semver and last_reviewed; reviewed at every release; rotation of obsolete steps logged.
rule.rationale: Smoke packs decay; periodic review removes obsolete steps and adds new critical paths.

rule.statement: Any step FAIL blocks promotion to production until either fixed or formally waived (named approver + recorded reason).
rule.rationale: Soft enforcement makes the gate decorative.

rule.statement: If full E2E suite under 5 min already gates promotion OR no RC stage exists OR no production users, skip this methodology.
rule.rationale: Smoke pack is wasted overhead in those configurations.
